questions.question: Which two sublayers live inside a single decoder transformer block?
options: Multi head causal attention訝럂 position wise MLP, each wrapped in a residual connection
questions.explanation: Every modern decoder LLM block is attention plus MLP, each with its own residual path訝럌ts own LayerNorm.

questions.question: What is the key difference between pre-LN訝럓ost-LN at depth?
options: Pre-LN keeps the residual signal unnormalized so gradients propagate cleanly to the embedding; post-LN puts the norm after the residual add, which shrinks gradients at depth訝럑eeds warmup
questions.explanation: Pre-LN trains stably without warmup at common learning rates; post-LN is what the 2017 paper shipped訝럑eeded warmup.

questions.question: What does the causal mask in multi head attention do?
options: Sets the upper triangle of the attention logits to negative infinity so token i cannot attend to tokens at positions greater than i
Picks the longest sequence in the batch
questions.explanation: Forgetting the causal mask trains a model that can read future tokens; the mask is the only piece that enforces causality.

questions.question: Why use a fused QKV projection instead of three separate linears?
options: One linear of width 3 times d_model collapses three kernel launches訝럗hree matmuls into one, faster on every accelerator訝럐atches the reference GPT-2 implementation
questions.explanation: Same math, fewer kernels; reference implementations of GPT-2, LLaMA,訝랱istral all ship the fused projection.

questions.question: Where should dropout NOT be placed in a transformer block?
options: On the residual identity path itself, since that breaks the additive identity that lets the gradient flow at depth
questions.explanation: Dropout belongs on the attention output訝럒n the MLP residual update, never on the identity branch of the residual.

questions.question: Why register the causal mask as a buffer rather than allocating it per forward call?
options: The mask depends only on the maximum context length, so allocating it once at construction訝럖licing the active window per call avoids an allocator hot spot at long context
questions.explanation: Allocate once with register_buffer, slice per forward; otherwise the mask becomes a per-call allocation cost.
